Tolan's Darkwood Breastplate  
 

Lore Item
Slot: CHEST
Charges: Unlimited
AC: 35
STR: +10 DEX: +10 WIS: +10 AGI: +10
Effect: Ring of Faydark (Any Slot/Can Equip, Casting Time: 30.0) at Level 45
WT: 10.0 Size: LARGE
Class: RNG
Race: HUM ELF HEF HFL DRK
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)

Charges: Unlimited

STR: +10 DEX: +10 WIS: +10 AGI: +10

This item has really come down in price, I just picked one up for 50k on Xegony. I bought this for the clickie as an alternative to buying gate-pots.
▲ Page top
50k, Feb-2008
QuoteReply
# Feb 29 2008 at 9:56 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1686529028957
Scholar
342 posts
In today's game, unless you have a lower level RNG or want a plate graphic, it is much more cost effective to buy gate potions.

Most folks bind in the Guild Lobby for ease in summon-rez-rebuff recovery. The Tolan BP takes you to Lesser Faydark requiring 3 zones to reach the Guild Lobby.

It is handy for porting after level 45 to L.Fay. Also, if money is no object and you want a RNG toy, by all means blow the coin. :)
▲ Page top
50k, Feb-2008
QuoteReply
# Nov 04 2009 at 2:38 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592347333397
Scholar
244 posts
another good thing about actually using the Tolan's is that
if you time it right you can hit Weapon Shield and then
click on the BP and be ported out by it before WS wears off.
A lifesaver for when I'm being swarmed by mobs.
I still carry it around with me just for that reason.
▲ Page top
Tolan's BP FAQs
QuoteReply
# Dec 30 2005 at 8:20 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592249973991
86 posts
1. Where does it port you? A: It ports you to Lesser Faydark next to the Greater Faydark zone line.

2. What is the cast time? A: 30 seconds

3. What does it look like when it is equipped? A: It has a plate look with a light brown color.

4. Is it worth 100k? A: You better believe it :)
▲ Page top
RE: Tolan's BP FAQs
QuoteReply
# May 09 2006 at 7:49 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592252480808
View User Forum
Scholar
19 posts
4. Worth 100k? I would say absolutly NOT

I mean come one, gate potions are what, 125pp a time to make thats around 800 gate potions before you reach that mark. Thats a LOT of gating

Yes it has nice stats on it to use from lvl 1 (I know the clickie doesn't) but there are better. By the time you can use it, you have better to wear also

Now you may well be able to argue why it is worth 100k, my opiniopn is its not. 30-50k MAX more like it, but then thats mu oppinion

▲ Page top
RE: Evac
QuoteReply
# Dec 31 2003 at 8:03 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592254498783
124 posts
Yup, thats exactly what I got it for.. always wanted one but by the time I could afford one I had better so it sits in my pack till I need to port. Wonderful for the Ranger who has ventured far from civilization and needs to get back quickly. On a side note tho, it CAN be interrupted... the trick is to have your back to something solid, that will cut down the chances of it happening. The only time mine was interrupted was when getting hit moved me back a few steps. I found a nifty little combo to ensure it works is tap weaponshield right b4 you click it and you will parry every attack from your front buying you just enough time to port safely! This BP rocks if your thinking about getting one, then do its worth it. If you already have one grats you know how precious it really is. :)
▲ Page top
Update
QuoteReply
# Dec 13 2003 at 12:44 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1593966285642
Sage
70 posts
Got it tonight on a raid I lead to Trak, didn't actually think I'd ever see it drop, but done a few anyway in case. Thanks to Arrowbearer for kindly rolling a 6 as the other ranger there =)

Few things I noticed / thoughts.

1. Combat use. The spell can be interrupted. It can be interrupted by things hitting you (though it seems rare so far) so perhaps this has been changed. Also, the SLIGHTEST movement will interrupt it - don't even turn left or right.

2. It ports you to the Gfay zoneline in Lesser Faydark - ie about 30 seconds away from the PoK book outside Felthwie. Which is pretty great, essentially a PoK port.

3. Best use apart from the norm seems to be in places like Seb where you may be a long way from the zoneline / locked in. Furthermore, you can now be a melee in a group of casters going to dangerous, gate-might-be-needed places without having a druid or wizzie there.

4. Best wipe use suggestions: If you're not wearing it (which you probably aren't at 65ish) ... Switch from bag, click Weaponshield on, cast BP. You now have 15 seconds of the 30 second cast time which are hp-loss free and interrupt free. Hope the other 15 seconds hold out =)

Only other thing that can better it is the third PoK Tali imo, and you have to be EP for that, and it's still a long and hard quest to get pieces for it. And until I get that, my BP will always be in my bag (ornate pips it stats-wise with the HP I'm afraid) in case I fall down a pit or something. Much happy tonight, thanks to the Dead Dwagon!
▲ Page top
i love.
QuoteReply
# Sep 19 2002 at 9:17 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592759368833
70 posts
dream come true. i just got this tonight and anyone who says the port is crap has never gone LD deep in seb or any other highlvl dongion so shudup this thing owns +P

Tolan BP Rules
QuoteReply
# Jul 16 2002 at 11:49 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592758942885
6 posts
The other night in Seb my BP got it's first real test. The zone crashed and I log back to find no grp, and a very empty dhall the the froggies found me. 3 of them on me heath going down fast what do you do. Clicked my BP and prayed I figured I was toast 3 mobs beating on me and i'm still taking hits. 5 seconds left i'm wating for the "spell interrupted" msg to show 1 bubble of hp left and I see it "Loading please wait...." Bam I'm in Lfay and I'm alive. Cheers to the Tolan BP



Edited, Thu Jul 18 19:39:45 2002

▲ Page top
Life Saver
QuoteReply
# May 24 2002 at 12:04 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592758349680
13 posts
I have this BP - It has saved my butt more than a few times. Say you're doing the Royals in Chardok and the tide turns against you - a wipe is imminent, Clerics are dead, Main Tank, SA TA either dead or dying, no chance of survival. Right click the BP and pray for 30 secs. BOOM - you are in LFay. Don't get me wrong, if you need to stand and die, you do it, but sometimes it's OK to port out and noone minds a bit. One less corpse to summon, one more for the rescue team - whatever.
Another nice deal - you are on a raid and RL pops up - you have to log - you are in PoH or Umbral Planes or Kael Arena, ToV, Faydedar raid, whereever (or the raid is over for that matter) - no looking for a wizzy to port you out, just click and go.
Advantage 3 - since SoL, it's easy to get anywhere (except WC) - just port to LFay, zone to either GFay for wizzy spires - Meet your wizzy friend for port or go anywhere via Nexus Port
(Velious is normally 3 minutes or less from the time you get to Nexus from GFay), or run to Steamfont Druid rings to meet your druid friend to hook up for a group.
Bottom line - this BP gives Rangers the freedom to port themselves - something I will NEVER give up. Even if I get a BP with better stats, I will keep this one just to Port at will.
If you aren't fortunate enough to be on a Trak raid where this drops and you get it, then by all means, if you ever get the chance RanJars, BUY IT - you will never regret it.
▲ Page top
RE: Life Saver
QuoteReply
# Jul 10 2002 at 4:25 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591770260749
Scholar
104 posts
Ever heard of the Thurgadin Gate potion? Easy to do, and yes, it is just *one* potion, but I keep 10 others in the bank 1 step away from the final combine (result is LORE, NO DROP). I have a basicly "free" port to Thugadin (also VERY close to the Luclin/Wiz spires) off of the Planes, or anywhere else that I can't get out of. Added bonus: The cast time is about as fast as a gate spell to cast. I'm in trouble soloing? Gonna die with a nasty CR? Pray that my roots stick on the mob(s) beating on me, then *poof*, back to Thurg. I talk to one NPC (close to the bank), do one combine (haven't failed on it yet, the pottery part I have, but this is just adding an NPC item, not using the wheel) and I am good to go, have another gate potion.

Farming the stuff out of crystal caverns to make this potion is easy. Orcs for velium, crystal spiders for crystaline silk, easily soloed at my level, and easily grouped earlier, look up the forums on CC. For the level this zone has great loot, plus a bank in zone. MADE for farming this stuff, no need to carry heavy velium around.

Pottery skill needed for this potion is 120 trivial. Getting pottery to 122 is a joke. Has to be the easist skill to master (at least up to there). The guide I used said "pottery to 122 in Thurgadin in under an hour for less than 20pp". The guide was right. In under an hour, for less than 20pp, I was ready for the ability to gate to Thurg for the rest of my EQ career (already had done the farming).

I was able to stay in the Plane of Hate when everyone camped for the night at the safespot, even though I wasn't sure if I would make it back in time the next day to meet up with everyone before we left for good. I can run to the Spires for a port to the nexus in a matter of minutes, even in the middle of the night when I can't find a port. I can go into zones I have no way out of and do some exploring, and know I can get out at any time.

If your faction isn't good enough to talk to the merchants in Thurg, sneak works well, if you have it (if you are a ranger, you have it). All the items to make the potion are farmed out of Crystal Caverns easily, which the entrance to is just a zone away from Thurg, so when I see supplies running low, I hop in there for a bit. Loot isn't bad there, so no biggie. I even can get the Blackened Crystaline Robe to sell, or the herbalist pack, or a few other decent sellable items while I'm there.

If you have this BP, grats, you are either in a good guild that hits that spot often, or you have a ton of money. Either way, why not have a port to BOTH Velious and Faydark? If you don't have this BP, do yourself a favor and make this potion, over and over. The BP you won't see for a long time, if at all, this potion can be made as soon as you are high enough level (late 20's?) to group in Crystal Caverns.

For the complete guide to make the gate potion, go here:

http://crpg.freehosting.net/shakasta/ThurgGatePotion.html

Safe Travels
▲ Page top

QuoteReply

▲ Page top
RE: lvl 7 girl with it
QuoteReply
# Nov 27 2001 at 11:35 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591752859896
View User Forum
Scholar
125 posts
twinking is a double-edged sword, and a practice that seems to have become much less frowned upon as the servers have aged.

on the one hand, the *real* victory of EQ, having reached 60, is suddenly finding oneself with amazing items, still slightly less powerful than currently weilded items, which are *not* no-drop. after spending many real-life months sitting in the sales zone of the server, bored, hoping for a "bite" on that high-end, mega-expensive item, eventually the temptation of bestowing a magnificent item upon one's own newly-created character becomes too hard to avoid. there's certainly something pimp about walking around, level 1-10, wearing a tolan's breastplate. (what's rarely realized however is that instead of magnanimous awe, the emotions observers over 30 are having are merely jealousy and a new sense of "well ***** this then, god, i work my *** off for a k-mart brand plywood lizardslayer and there's a level 10 over there with a geldarf's staff of amazing wondertude" and typically they log off and commit suicide.) to some that's not nice behavior. others like to inspire this feeling in others.

the other side of the coin is this. aside from causing others to look at you jealously, you now have a character without many goals other than leveling. since this character can be spoon-fed practically any item he or she wants by daddy, the art of the quest is now kind of boring. sure, it's an "all-new class" with "all new quests" but who cares? you're wearing tolan's darkwood chestplate. you sure you want to replace it with ivy etched once you easily complete the quest?

my ranger twinked my chanter at the beginning. i felt like i'd earned at least that much ability. i got him some ghostly robes, a staff of writhing, and a few other things. nothing uber certainly, but there truly is nothing like the utter awe on the faces of the literal newbies around you. for them the game is further inspired. they don't know what a "twink" is yet, all they know is there's a guy standing there with a staff with a SKULL on it! and the items certainly allowed for a faster leveling process through levels of the game which are NOT fun if you've done them before. there is NOTHING cool about getting killed by a fire beetle, okay? running away from a bat is LAME, alright?

anyway, there's my take on twinking. what the hell, right?
▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: Oct 30 2001 at 5:51 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) a good bp is also ceremonial iksar (as far as i kno rangers cna wear it altho it just migfht be soem dumass lieing to me) or TV stuff or afungi if your realy rich hell i dont care if AC is king and i got a 30 AC bp cholorplast will make up for it
Port location
QuoteReply
# Oct 15 2001 at 6:16 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591766340306
6 posts
Just to end the confusion, a right-click on this bp ports you into LESSER Faydark right near the GFay zoneline.

It probably won't save you from an attack because the cast time is quite slow, but it can get you out of zones with difficult-to-reach exits.

Self-porting for a ranger is a dream come true even if it is to Lfay. It is only a few steps away from most ranger's home, not to mention a wiz portal and a bank.
▲ Page top
RE: Port location
QuoteReply
# Jan 31 2002 at 6:00 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593361662060
196 posts
Something not specifically noted is that the wizard spire in GFay is Nexus capable. A ranger equipped with this BP therefore has resonably low travel time to GFay, Nexus, NK, Toxx and Great Divide. Grats to the treehuggers :)

Nenelyan
Wizard
Torv

What is the drop rate of the BP
QuoteReply
# Oct 01 2001 at 4:37 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592249533303
20 posts
I am nearing 45 now, and I think my Ivy is getting a bit rusty from all those days in the snow and rain. This obviously would make an amazing upgrade to my armor, but alas i dont' have near enough for what people would want for this. So here are my questions
1. What is the drop rate of Tolan's BP?
2. What is a good level to start getting a group together to take Trakanon?
3. What would be a good armor upgrade in the meantime.
▲ Page top
RE: What is the drop rate of the BP
QuoteReply
# Oct 29 2001 at 3:44 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591761991555
Scholar
300 posts
1. If you take a look at the drops for Trak, you can imagine that the Tolan BP isn't all that common. Add to that the fact that Trak isn't a mob that's killed 3 times a day, and you get a very, very rare BP. Not to mention that this BP would probably stay in whatever guild happened to get the drop instead of getting sold.
2. Trak is level 65, so it depends on what kind of guild support you have. 55+ would be a good time to think about it.
3. Although the Ivy Etched Tunic is probably the most useful of all the IE pieces, there are several upgrades you can look for. Treeweave immediately comes to mind. 25 AC 10 STR is pretty good for around 2kpp, and a lot of Rangers choose to go with it. For something with better stats but less AC, the Seb Scale Coat is good. 22 AC 6 STR 6 CHA 6 WIS 2 SvD/C/M. Much more expensive at around 4-5kpp. If you've got more cash to burn, consider the Spined Seahorse Hide BP (29 AC 15 DEX 50 HP) or the Barbed Ringmail BP (30 AC 9 STR 50 HP).

Trakanon would simply blink one eye halfway at you, and the skinwould fall away from your bones as you get hit for 999,999,999,999,999,999,999 points of damage. What would inspire you to ask if a 30 something character could get an item from a 60+ mob? Crack? An obsession with death? A yearning to see the afterlife before anyone else?

HELL NO YOU CAN'T GET IT AT YOUR LEVEL!!!!!!!

The Port
QuoteReply
# Sep 01 2001 at 1:10 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591766563831
7 posts
Ok, first off, if you wanna know what type of port it is, you have to look at the name. Wizard ports are gates, portals, and translocates. Druids ports are rings and circles. If the pic is correct, than its a druid port to gfay/lfay, not sure which. both lfay and gfay have abandoned druid rings, so it could be either one. to the person that said you could get this BP at lvl 15, i serioucly hope you were joking, cuz this is a trak BP, so u wont get it unless ur twinked/lvl 50+ and have a guild that can kill trak. and if you dont like this Bp, then why post at all? it just wastes your time and the time of other people reading your post. hapy hunting =D
